prompt:
Wire classic-laddie up as a valley host: consume the-valley's NixOS module to serve bare-git hosting, with GitHub as a transitional push mirror, plus a default-off restic offsite backup layer. This implements the infra half of the-valley's outcome oc-9949561 (see github:gunk-dev/the-valley, .the-valley/outcomes/oc-9949561-push-replication.md and decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md: decided mechanism = push-triggered git mirror + nightly restic; the Hetzner VPS/Storage Box are NOT provisioned yet, so anything needing them stays documented-but-disabled).

CONTEXT YOU NEED (verified):
- the-valley (github:gunk-dev/the-valley) exports nixosModules.valley-host (nix/valley-host.nix). Option surface: services.valley = { enable; user (default 'git', git-shell, key-only); group; dataDir (default /srv/git — also the git user's home, so clone URLs are git@host:<name>.git); authorizedKeys (list of SSH pubkeys, MUST be non-empty or an assertion fails); config (path to a CUE 'package valley' host declaration, vetted at build time against the shipped schema) }. The module creates bare repos via a valley-init oneshot, wires a post-receive dispatcher plus per-project best-effort 'git push --mirror' hooks. Mirror CREDENTIALS are deliberately the consumer's concern: the module assumes the git user's SSH identity and known_hosts are provisioned by the host config.
- cosmo PR #601 ('feat(git-hosting): declarative bare-git hosting on classic-laddie') is OPEN but SUPERSEDED by this approach per the-valley's decision node dcr-0f5d9b1. Read #601 briefly for anything worth salvaging (e.g. dataset/dataDir choices), but do NOT reuse its implementation; your PR body should state it supersedes #601.
- classic-laddie config: hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix; secrets are agenix (.age files in secrets/, recipients in secrets/keys.nix + secrets/secrets.nix); the repo already has patterns for 'add the secret entry + comment documenting the agenix -e command to populate it' (see the recyclarr/reel-life comments in classic-laddie/default.nix). classic-laddie auto-rebuilds from cosmo main on push (cosmo-rebuild service), so whatever merges will deploy itself — everything must be safe to deploy before the human follow-ups are done.

THE WORK:
1. Add flake input the-valley.url = "github:gunk-dev/the-valley" (inputs.nixpkgs.follows = "nixpkgs") and pass it through like the existing reel-life/github-relay module inputs.
2. Create the host declaration hosts/classic-laddie/valley.cue (package valley) declaring project "the-valley" with mirrors: ["git@github.com:gunk-dev/the-valley.git"]. This mirrors gunk-dev/the-valley on GitHub as the transitional mirror per the-valley's roadmap Phase 0. Validate it: nix run nixpkgs#cue -- vet -c <the-valley checkout>/schema/valley.cue hosts/classic-laddie/valley.cue (fetch the schema from the flake input store path or a shallow clone).
3. In hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix: import inputs.the-valley.nixosModules.valley-host; enable services.valley with config = ./valley.cue and authorizedKeys = the patrick user keys already listed in secrets/keys.nix (import that file rather than duplicating key literals if reasonably clean). Keep the default dataDir /srv/git unless #601 or the host's ZFS layout (tank pool exists) argues clearly for a dedicated dataset — if it does, prefer documenting the dataset as a follow-up rather than depending on a dataset that doesn't exist yet; the deploy must not fail on first boot.
4. Mirror credentials plumbing: add an agenix secret (e.g. secrets/valley-git-ssh-key.age, owner = the git user, mode 0400) for the git user's SSH private key, and wire the git user's SSH config so the post-receive mirror push can authenticate to github.com: provision ~git/.ssh (in dataDir) with a config/IdentityFile pointing at the decrypted secret path and a known_hosts entry for github.com (use the well-known github.com ssh-ed25519 host key, pinned, not ssh-keyscan at runtime). Follow the repo's existing bind-mount/tmpfiles/ownership patterns; the git user is a system user with git-shell — do not give it a real shell. Document in comments the human steps: generate the keypair, agenix -e secrets/valley-git-ssh-key.age, add the PUBLIC key as a deploy key WITH WRITE ACCESS on github.com/gunk-dev/the-valley. Until that's done a mirror push just fail-logs (the hook is best-effort by design) — that is acceptable and should be stated in the PR body.
5. Restic offsite layer, default OFF: add a small module or config block (e.g. hosts/classic-laddie/valley-backup.nix or inline) using services.restic.backups to back up the valley dataDir nightly to a Hetzner Storage Box over sftp, with passwordFile + repository/credentials from agenix secrets (secrets/valley-restic-password.age, secrets/valley-restic-repo.age or an environmentFile — pick the cleanest fit with services.restic's options). Gate it behind an explicit enable that defaults to false with a comment: 'enable after the Hetzner Storage Box is provisioned; see dcr-db1acbb'. Include sensible prune/keep policy and a comment documenting the agenix -e commands.
6. Register any new secrets in secrets/secrets.nix with the right recipient keys (classic-laddie host key + patrick user keys, matching existing entries).

TESTS (run before PR): follow cosmo's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md guidance; at minimum 'nix flake check' and an eval of the host closure: nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath (this catches module/option errors without building the full CUDA-heavy closure). Also run the cue vet from step 2 explicitly. If the repo has additional standard checks (pre-commit hooks), run them.

PR BODY must include: what this implements (oc-9949561 infra half, decision dcr-db1acbb), supersedes #601, the exact human follow-up checklist (generate+populate valley-git-ssh-key, add GitHub deploy key with write access, provision Hetzner VPS + Storage Box, populate restic secrets, then flip the backup enable and add the Hetzner mirror URL to valley.cue), and the note that mirror pushes fail-log harmlessly until the deploy key exists.
